title: Anvil Workbench plugin receipt v1
description: The typed, redacted receipt the plugin host returns for one plugin request (a tool call or a lifecycle action). It echoes the request's `request_digest` and reports one outcome: `accepted` (the effect completed), `duplicate` (an idempotent replay of an already-completed request), `denied` (a fail-closed preflight refusal), or `reconcile` (an unknown outcome for an in-flight external effect, per the declared cancellation policy — R012). It is an audit-preserving record: every object is closed, credential use is reported by opaque host-owned reference only (never a value — R004), and every human-readable field is a bounded non-leaking safe string, so no endpoint, path, credential, or unredacted tool payload is representable. All prose is untrusted display data. This is a hub-durable audit record, not a contract-digest-bearing snapshot.

safeText.description: A bounded, human-readable string held to a structurally constrained best-effort denylist: the negative lookahead refuses common URL, protocol-relative, IPv4, and scheme-less host:port shapes — including a dotless single-label service:port such as a compose service name (the lowercase-anchored `[a-z][a-z0-9-]*:\d{2,5}` alternative; the lowercase anchor deliberately spares an ISO timestamp's `T09:15` and a hex `sha256:` digest) — plus filesystem-path, backslash, connection-string, and credential/key shapes (AWS key ids, JWTs, PEM headers, bearer/authorization/api-key/secret/token markers, and .env/.pem-style cred file suffixes) so a routine receipt line cannot ferry a raw endpoint, path, or token into the UI. It is a best-effort structural backstop, not exhaustive and not a proof of unrepresentability — redaction remains the emitting plugin host's obligation; this pattern is a defence-in-depth boundary, not the primary redaction one.

credential_use.description: R004: credential use is reported by opaque host-owned reference only. There is no value, token, key, or password field; the browser and model never receive credential material.

result.description: Present on accepted/duplicate. The tool output is reduced to an opaque digest plus a bounded safe summary; the raw payload never enters the receipt.

error.description: Present on denied. A stable code plus a human-safe summary.

reconciliation.description: Present on reconcile (R012): an in-flight external effect with an unknown outcome, routed to reconciliation rather than reported as a false success.
